incidents.vector: Attackers compromised Stryker's Microsoft Intune device management console and remotely triggered mass factory-reset wipes across enrolled corporate devices worldwide, without deploying malware.
incidents.impact: Over 200,000 devices wiped across factories in the US, Ireland, India, and other countries; employees locked out of critical systems for over a week. FBI and CISA released joint Intune hardening advisory. FBI seized the group's website under a seizure warrant citing coordination with a foreign state actor.

incidents.victim: Multiple organizations (government, healthcare, education — Cisco Firewall Management Center customers)
incidents.attacker: Criminal gang — ransomware (Interlock group, possible ties to Rhysida operation)
incidents.vector: Exploitation of Cisco Secure Firewall Management Center zero-day (CVE-2026-20131) beginning January 26, over five weeks before public disclosure on March 4; Amazon CISO published findings from a misconfigured Interlock staging server.
incidents.impact: Confirmed victims include DaVita (dialysis), a major Ohio healthcare system, the city of St. Paul MN (National Guard called in for recovery), and multiple K-12 schools. Operators use regulatory exposure threats for extortion. CISA added CVE to KEV catalog with emergency patch mandate.

incidents.victim: State Hydrographic Service of Ukraine (maritime navigation agency)
incidents.vector: Phishing email exploiting a cross-site scripting flaw in Zimbra webmail (CVE-2025-66376); the entire attack payload was embedded in the email HTML body with no attachments or links, sent from a compromised student account in January.
incidents.impact: Attackers silently harvested login credentials, session tokens, two-factor authentication backup codes, browser-stored passwords, and up to 90 days of mailbox data from the maritime navigation agency.

incidents.victim: Google Chrome and Chromium-based browser users worldwide
incidents.vector: Exploitation of two zero-day vulnerabilities in Google Chrome: a V8 engine flaw (CVE-2026-3910) and a Skia graphics library out-of-bounds write (CVE-2026-3909).
incidents.impact: CISA added both vulnerabilities to KEV catalog based on active exploitation; could allow remote code execution through crafted web content. Federal agencies ordered to patch.

incidents.vector: Exploitation of an authentication bypass vulnerability in Ivanti Endpoint Manager (CVE-2026-1603), allowing unauthorized access to endpoint management infrastructure.
incidents.impact: CISA added to KEV catalog based on active exploitation; enables attackers to bypass authentication and potentially gain control of managed endpoints across an organization.

incidents.vector: Exploitation of an information disclosure vulnerability in Wing FTP Server (CVE-2025-47813).
incidents.impact: CISA added to KEV catalog based on active exploitation; allows attackers to access sensitive information from affected FTP servers.

incidents.victim: Organizations using n8n workflow automation platform
incidents.vector: Exploitation of improper control of dynamically-managed code resources in n8n (CVE-2025-68613), enabling remote code execution.
incidents.impact: CISA added to KEV catalog based on active exploitation; allows attackers to execute arbitrary code on n8n instances used for workflow automation.

incidents.victim: Organizations using SolarWinds Web Help Desk and Omnissa Workspace ONE
incidents.vector: Active exploitation of a deserialization of untrusted data vulnerability in SolarWinds Web Help Desk (CVE-2025-26399) and a server-side request forgery in Omnissa Workspace ONE (CVE-2021-22054).
incidents.impact: CISA added both to KEV catalog; SolarWinds flaw enables remote code execution on help desk servers, Omnissa flaw allows server-side request forgery on enterprise mobility management systems.
